subtitle: 01 / GREETING
title: ANTHOLOGY
paragraphs: I draw because some things are easier to understand with a pencil than with words.

This archive contains sketches made from curiosity, boredom, admiration, stubbornness, late-night impulses, and the occasional challenge I should have probably ignored. Some pieces were carefully planned. Others began with a simple thought: "this might be fun to draw."

The collection moves between portrait studies, cross-hatching experiments, character sketches, and whatever else managed to occupy my attention long enough to make it onto paper. Every line records a decision, every shadow a small negotiation between patience and frustration.

Sometimes a sketch exists because a face was interesting, a composition felt right, or I simply wanted to see if I could pull it off.

Consider this website an archive of observations, experiments, and moments of fixation—proof that given enough time, graphite, ink, and questionable levels of determination, almost anything can become a drawing.
